question: What is the name of the University that the students have been accepted to?
answer: Ahmedabad University.
contexts:
## Ahmedabad University Students Events and Activities Committee (AUSEAC)  
Student life is not just about coursework - it's about creativity, connection, and making memories that shape who we become. That's where our student clubs step in. They offer spaces to experiment, lead, laugh, learn, and leave your mark. Behind the scenes, AUSEAC - the Ahmedabad University Student Events and Activities Committee- helps this vibrant ecosystem thrive.  
Functioning under the Office of the Dean of Students (ODS), AUSEAC isn't just a procedural body - it's a pulse-checker, a sounding board, and a creative enabler for all clubs across the University. It also establishes criteria for creating new student clubs and grants approval for their formation.  
List of names of AUSEAC members:

## Student Clubs  
At Ahmedabad University, our clubs are not just small groups of students with a common interest. They represent a world of passion, imagination, teamwork, and discovery. Whether it's music, theatre, tech, community work, or pure fun, clubs let students come together, take the lead, and do things that are tried and tested, and also create innovative events that haven't been done before. They're spaces to grow, connect, and create something meaningful.  
The University encourages students to take the initiative to start new clubs based on any new areas of interest. Proposals for these should be discussed with AUSEAC.  
1. Astronomy Club
2. Dance Club
3. Design Club
4. Entrepreneurs' Club
5. Environment Club
6. Film Appreciation Club
7. Fine Arts Club
8. Fitness Club
9. Food and Nutrition Club
10. Heritage Club
11. Literary Club

## American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME)  
The ASME Ahmedabad University Student Association is a student-run organisation affiliated with the global ASME network. It is committed to nurturing professional development and cultivating a passion for mechanical engineering.

## Ingenium  
Ingenium, organised by the School of Engineering and Applied Science,  is  the  annual  technology  festival  of  Ahmedabad University.  Started  in  2016,  Ingenium  celebrates  the  spirit of innovation and  engineering  solutions, hosting various competitions and workshops. It applies the University's core  values  of  project-based  learning  and  interdisciplinary approach to real-life problems.

question: What are the four sectors of impact that Ahmedabad University recognises as influencing social challenges and job opportunities?
answer: health, transport, energy, and education
contexts:
## Sustainability  
Climate change and biodiversity loss are global challenges that are affecting the wellbeing of the planet and the people. There  is  a  need  for  a  clear  response  from  governments, organisations, and  businesses  to  tackle  these  issues. Ahmedabad  University  is  confronting  these  challenges through teaching and research and by transforming these into actions towards attaining a sustainable world.  
The University has a deeper mandate to actively advance the social, economic, and ecological developments of local, national,  and  international  communities  by  educating and  empowering  students  to  become  leaders  working towards enabling a sustainable future. The centrepiece of Ahmedabad University's sustainability strategy is to act as a  platform  aimed  at  learning,  practising,  and  advancing sustainability. The strategy emerges from this framework and  signifies  the  next  step  in  the  University's  evolving commitment to ensure sustainability. It is also our pathway for building and operating a more sustainable campus.

## Our Purpose  
Ahmedabad University  is dedicated to foster continuous progress of self and society. We aspire to transform higher education and research in India. Our graduates have the capabilities, attitudes, and values to excel in any field they pursue around the world.  
- To prepare leaders of outstanding character who will contribute significantly to their fields of study and practice
- To build an evolving learning environment that is based on cross-disciplinary linkages between the arts, sciences, and professional disciplines, combined with rigour and reflection
- To actively advance research and intellectual enterprise at the University and in society
- To promote independent-mindedness and diversity across all dimensions of the University
- To generate knowledge that provides a context of learning from and contribution to India and the world
- To  actively  advance  the  social,  economic,  and  ecological  development  of  local,  national,  and  international communities
- To educate young people of India and the world to become contextually-literate global citizens
- To  catalyse  students  to  mature  into  critical  thinkers  who  are  analytically  equipped,  practically  oriented,  and ethically driven  
We recognise that social challenges and job opportunities are occurring at the intersection of various axes of influence, defined by disciplines (data, materials, biology, and behaviour), nature (air, water, forests, and land), sectors of impact (health, transport, energy, and education) and society (individual and community). Accordingly, Ahmedabad University strives to guide students on how to learn through interdisciplinary academics and real-life experiences that traverse these intersections. The research programmes at the University also embody this integrative philosophy.  
<!-- image -->

## Dear Students,  
Congratulations on your acceptance to Ahmedabad University!  
We are happy to welcome you to a rich academic environment, characterised by a wide choice and flexibility in course selections, and a distinctive interdisciplinary approach. Integral to your University life will also be a wide array of extra-curricular offerings, including student-run clubs and a strong sports programme supported by exceptional indoor and outdoor amenities.  
We urge you to immerse yourself in University life and make the most of the opportunities available. The University cares deeply for your growth and development into intelligent and compassionate citizens of the twenty-first century, and strives to enable you to respond to the challenges of our times.  
It is a brave new world, and what you do in the University will help you discover what you love to do and, through this, your most meaningful and fulfilling relationship with society and the world. All of us - young and old - must aim to become transformative agents in this emerging global order, equipped with critical thinking capabilities, a liberal outlook, and a profound insight into life, truth, and beauty.  
They used to say it takes a village to raise a child. There is great truth in that age-old adage, which bears mulling over. Similarly, it takes a committed, caring, and liberal University to raise intelligent and compassionate citizens of the world. Ahmedabad University is steadfastly committed to providing all the avenues required for facilitating your growth, and enabling your access to these avenues. This is why you must carefully consider the following three things that you may have already imbibed as a student: your commitment to be engaged with all of University life, your desire to learn and pursue excellence in whatever you choose to do, and your responsibility to help and enable those around you, including your peers at the University, the people of Ahmedabad, and the world at large.  
Please  do  not  hesitate  to  reach  out  to  us  at  the  Office  of  the  Dean  of  Students  (ODS).  Every  single physical, emotional, or social challenge that you may experience is of concern and interest to the ODS, and we will do our best to help you in dealing with these challenges. We keenly look forward to getting to know you individually.  
We are with you in your thoughts and feelings as you enter this exciting new phase of your life.  
Welcome aboard!

## Dear Students,  
Welcome  to  Ahmedabad.  We  believe  that  the  role  of  an  undergraduate education  is  to  create  well-rounded  thinking  citizens  who  will  make meaningful contributions to society. Our curriculum is designed to achieve this  goal  with  a  unique  combination  of  breadth  and  depth,  making  it challenging and engaging.  
Every undergraduate student at Ahmedabad University participates in the common core in the form of the Foundation Programme. This is a one-ofits-kind programme in the world that exposes you to societal issues through experiential learning. Last year, we also introduced the First Year Interdisciplinary Seminars in Critical Thinking and Writing. These courses encourage you to build upon your Foundation Programme knowledge, think critically, and ask meaningful questions.  
You will learn that no single discipline can solve complex problems facing humanity,  and  collaboration  between  different  disciplines  is  essential  to solve these problems. You will also take thoughtfully designed core courses in your Major that lay the foundation for deeper enquiry in these areas.  
In  addition,  you  will  take  courses  outside  your  Major  to  satisfy  General Education  Requirements.  These  will  allow  you  to  pursue  interests  outside your Major and introduce you to new areas of interest. You can also be able to  pursue a Minor in a different area along with your Major. This flexibility allows you to tailor your programme to your interests and long term career goals.  
We believe in a hands-on approach to learning. In the final year of your programme,  you  will  have  the  opportunity  to  pursue  an  Undergraduate Thesis or a Capstone Project. This will enable you to apply your learnings at the University to address a research question or solve a real-life problem.  
We  are  confident  that  you  will  enjoy  your  time  at  the  University.  The programmes  have  been  carefully  crafted  to  enable  you  to  face  the challenges of an ever-changing world confidently. We hope you will take full advantage of the myriad opportunities the University offers, and spread your wings and fly high.

question: What is one of the ways in which recent batches of students at Ahmedabad University have been studying the history of science?
answer: Through graphic novels.

## ACADEMIC LIFE  
At  Ahmedabad  University,  instead  of  focusing  on  the traditional  lecture-based  method,  our  emphasis  is  on teaching students to think for themselves, link theoretical schemes to living contexts, and bring one's concepts to enable critical thinking for solving real-world challenges. We  develop  novel,  experimental  teaching  methods  to revitalise the classroom experience for a new generation of students. To achieve this, our faculty have customised a  series  of  innovative  courses.  For  instance,  recent batches  have  been  studying  the  history  of  science through graphic novels, learning physics through constructing musical instruments, and being introduced to  forensic  biology  by  crafting  and  solving  simulated crime scenes.  
Interdisciplinarity  is  both  a  core  value  and  a  guiding principle  central  to  Ahmedabad  University  since  its inception. Our vision is to create a unique system where our  students  can  actively  shape  their  intellectual  and educational trajectories by utilising the full range of the University's  offerings.  We  believe  that  instead  of  being pushed  into  narrow  and  streamlined  specialisations, students will thrive in an atmosphere where they are  exposed  to  diverse  perspectives  and  receive  a comprehensive, well-rounded education.  
Positive and respectful interactions with a diverse group  of  peers  can  enhance  one's  understanding  of self and others and the ability to work in teams, as well as  inculcate  open-mindedness,  which  is  crucial  for developing innovative ideas.  
We  are committed  to fostering a welcoming and respectful environment where people from diverse backgrounds can attain their full potential.

Ahmedabad University focuses on teaching students independent thinking  and  connecting  theory  to  real-world  issues. We have also partnered with the US-based Olin College of Engineering in implementing project-based learning and design thinking to build capabilities and a practice-oriented mindset among students.

## Dear Students,  
We are delighted to have you as students at Ahmedabad University. Doctoral and Masters students are an important part of our student community.  During  your  education  here,  you  will  have  several opportunities to take courses in diverse areas to help you broaden your outlook and improve your skills.  
Our University is making all efforts to provide a holistic education that will be relevant in the 21 st  century, so that you can make an effective contribution towards solving societal problems that usually require inputs from different disciplines.  
The courses in our University encourage you to think and to tinker, not just to memorise and reproduce. Our faculty are involved in both fundamental and applied research in  diverse  areas.  Many  of  the research themes are focused on solving socially relevant problems. Besides academic development, at Ahmedabad University you will also be able to interact with renowned experts from other disciplines that  will  enable  you  to  build  professional  contacts. ## Foundation Programme  
All  students  entering  the  undergraduate  programme  go through  our  common  core,  the  Foundation  Programme,  in the  first  year.  This  builds  the  foundations  of  interdisciplinary learning  and  engagement  with  issues  of  society  through project-based  learning.  The  programme  is  built  around  six domains  that  define  contemporary  academic  and  life  skills in  the  21 st century,  areas  of  innovation  that  are  transforming the  world,  and  perspectives  that  help  students  become responsible members of any society. This includes:  
Students  develop  a  distinctive  approach  to  personal  and professional life by engaging with these domains. The foundational elements of these domains are delivered through three thematic studios, helping students imbibe a holistic and hands-on  approach  to  thinking,  enquiry,  and  action.  They learn  to  understand  multiple  issues  that  define  a  problem, learn  the  art  and  science  of  synthesis,  and  design  solutions for  the  challenges.  Every  student  participates  in  these  three studios.  
The  following  are  the  three  designated  Thematic  Studios offered in 2025-26:  
- Democracy and Justice
- Environment and Climate Change
- Water  
In  addition  to  the  three  studios,  all  students  will  also  take  a First-year Interdisciplinary Seminar, focusing on reading, writing,  and  critical  thinking.  Offered  in  a  studio  format,  this seminar will be considered the fourth studio of the Foundation Programme.  The  student  chooses  one  course  from  a  list  of such studio courses offered in the winter semester.  
The Foundation Programme will contribute 12 credits* to the total number of credits. The Foundation Programme studio on Environment  and  Climate  Change  satisfies  all  requirements for pursuing a course on the environment.

## Experiential Learning  
Ahmedabad University focuses on teaching students independent thinking  and  connecting  theory  to  real-world  issues.  We  use innovative,  hands-on  teaching  methods,  like  learning  science through graphic novels or physics by building instruments. We have also partnered with the US-based Olin College of Engineering in implementing project-based learning and design thinking to build capabilities and a practice-oriented mindset among students.

## Undergraduate Programme  
The undergraduate programme  imparts skills, promotes citizenship, builds courage, and inculcates responsibility among students. It provides a broad and deep education that will equip you to navigate the world you live in and become an agent of change for the betterment of your life and society. It will help you discover your passion in life and prepare you to pursue it while also building a deep expertise in your chosen field. Our ambition is to facilitate the crossover of disciplinary learning and create a pathway towards earning a livelihood.  
Empathy, social contribution, and a commitment to intellectual  freedom  define  our  University  environment.  We endorse scholarship as a means to address conflicting ideas respectfully, ensuring all voices are heard and protected as a core part of the undergraduate experience. The undergraduate curriculum enables you to develop arguments cogently and express yourself effectively, both verbally and in writing.

question: What is the name of one of the pre-designed interdisciplinary majors in the Bachelor of Arts Programme?
answer: Philosophy, History and Languages.
contexts:
## Programmes, Major and Minor  
The University offers undergraduate and integrated programmes leading to various degrees. Within each programme, a student chooses a discipline  to  specialise  in, known as a major. For instance, in a BA (Honours), a student may select  a  major  such  as  History  or  Economics.  Similarly, a  student  can  choose  a  major  in  Finance  or  Operations Management  from  the  Bachelor  of  Science  in  Management (Honours) programme.  
In addition to a major, students may select an optional minor. A minor is an alternative area of specialisation outside one's major discipline, and it cannot be in the same field or be closely related to the major. The purpose of a minor is to facilitate the development of alternative areas of expertise. While declaring a  major  is  mandatory,  students  are  encouraged  to  declare a minor. Prerequisites for majors or minors may be specified regarding preparation and academic achievement.  
The University also offers exceptionally well-prepared students the  flexibility  to  create  a  customised  major.  Such  a  student may opt to undertake an Interdisciplinary Major (instead of a pre-defined major) or a Self-designed Major.  
Students must initiate a request for an interdisciplinary major and  defend  a  proposal  before  a  Committee  on  Individual Choice. If approved, the programme of study will be designed by  the  Committee  on  Individual  Choice  in  conjunction  with the  student.  An  interested  student  should  apply  to  a  single major  at  the  time  of  application  and  may  indicate  their interest in an Interdisciplinary Major. The student will work with the Committee on Individual Choice during their first year at the  University,  designing  the  interdisciplinary  major  and  its accompanying programme of study by the end of the second semester. Alternatively, students may apply to transfer to an interdisciplinary major at any time; however, this may require additional  courses,  potentially  increasing  the  programme's duration. (Note that this option is in addition to the pre-designed interdisciplinary majors at the University, such as Philosophy, History and Languages, or Social and Political Sciences in the Bachelor of Arts Programme, or Finance and Economics in the Bachelor of Science in Management Programme.)

## School of Arts and Sciences  
The School of Arts and Sciences has five divisions. These are:  
- Biological and Life Sciences,
- Humanities and Languages,
- Mathematical and Physical Sciences,
- Performing and Visual Arts, and
- Social Sciences.  
The undergraduate programmes offer students the opportunity  to  take  courses  spanning  these  divisions  as well  as  from  other  Schools  and  Centres  at  the  University. The  School  provides  a  rigorous  foundation  for  students  in the  Major  of  their  choice.  However,  all  the  programmes  at the School emphasise interdisciplinary, research-based teaching to foster a spirit of innovation and discovery.

## General Education Requirements  
The General Education Requirements build the breadth further while starting to understand the disciplines' methods that  help  build  intellectual  enquiry,  systems  of  knowledge, and  skills  from  various  disciplines.  It  allows  for  additional exploration  stemming  from  the  introduction  to  disciplines in  the  Foundation  Programme.  It  also  allows  the  student  to discover their passion and interests further.  
The following will make up the General Education Requirements

- Humanities and Languages

- Mathematical and Physical Sciences

(the above courses can be in the major area)

question: Can students change their majors to one hosted by a different School at the University?
answer: Yes, students can change their majors to one hosted by a different School at the University, subject to eligibility requirements and with the approval of the Dean of the School hosting the new major.
contexts:
## Flexibility in the Ahmedabad University Undergraduate Programme  
Most undergraduate students still discover their passion upon entering  the  University.  Hence,  our  undergraduate  period allows for this discovery through flexibility in programmes and curriculum, creating an environment that develops a love for lifelong learning. Towards that,  
- We ensure that all courses are open to all students, subject to meeting the prerequisites
- We  allow  students  to  change  their  majors,  including moving to a major hosted by a different School, subject to  eligibility  requirements  and  with  the  approval  of  the Dean of  the  School  hosting  the  new  major.  However,  as time  progresses  in  a  programme,  a  change  in  major may  extend  your  stay  duration  since  the  new  major's requirements  must  be  completed.  Not  all  courses  (or none)  in  the  old  major  may  count  for  the  new  major's requirements.  
You can also choose to enter the University with an Undeclared Major  and  take  courses  at  the  University  to  determine  your passion  and  capabilities.  Thereafter,  select  a  major  of  your choice, subject to eligibility requirements and with the approval of the Dean of the School hosting the major.  
at  the  University.  Volunteer  work  could  include  teaching underprivileged children, participating in adult literacy programmes,  distributing  blankets  or  other  items,  working with old age homes and orphanages, providing support and encouragement  to  patients  at  hospitals,  cleaning  heritage sites,  engaging  with  workers  on  campus,  operating  a  food bank,  engaging  with  the  police,  municipal  government,  zoo, sanitation services, etc.  
*The total number of credits are under review and are subject to change.

## Change of Programme or Major  
The eligibility requirements for changing or declaring a major may include a minimum performance in the relevant courses and the inclusion of certain subjects in the student's academic programme in high school. Switching between BTech majors will adhere to existing government regulations. Due to Gujarat government regulations, transfers from non-BTech majors to BTech majors are not permitted.  
Students generally declare their major by the fourth semester. However,  you  can  change  your  major  at  any  time,  provided you  complete  your  undergraduate  programme  within  the 10-year  statutory  limit.  Changing  majors  or  starting  with  an undeclared  major  might  extend  the  time  it  takes  to  get  a degree.  
Consult Major Advisors or Programme Chairs about changing or selecting a major or programme. They can provide details on specific eligibility requirements.

## Major Requirements  
A  major  signifies  the  specialisation  area  within  a  degree (e.g.,  BA  in  History,  BTech  in  Chemical  and  Environmental Engineering, or BSM in Economics and Finance). The student can choose a major upon entering the University but has the flexibility  to  change  the  major  as  per  the  prescribed  policy. Some  Schools  may  require  more  credits  for  a  major  than the  80  credits*  mentioned  above  (in  which  case,  the  total requirements for the degree will be higher than 173 credits*). The University also admits students as Undecided - those who would like to choose a programme or major once they take some courses.  
Please consult the credit requirements in the Programme and Major sections.  
A Major may have the following structure:  
- School Core
- Programme Core
- Major or Discipline Core
- Major or Discipline Electives
- Internships
- Undergraduate  Thesis  or  Capstone  Project  or  a  BTech Project with a Writing Component  
Students can earn 12 credits* for their Undergraduate Thesis or Capstone Project, whether undertaken within or outside the University. For some projects, these credits can be spread over two semesters.  
*The total number of credits are under review and are subject to change.

## Programmes, Major and Minor  
The University offers undergraduate and integrated programmes leading to various degrees. Within each programme, a student chooses a discipline  to  specialise  in, known as a major. For instance, in a BA (Honours), a student may select  a  major  such  as  History  or  Economics.  Similarly, a  student  can  choose  a  major  in  Finance  or  Operations Management  from  the  Bachelor  of  Science  in  Management (Honours) programme.  
In addition to a major, students may select an optional minor. A minor is an alternative area of specialisation outside one's major discipline, and it cannot be in the same field or be closely related to the major. The purpose of a minor is to facilitate the development of alternative areas of expertise. While declaring a  major  is  mandatory,  students  are  encouraged  to  declare a minor. Prerequisites for majors or minors may be specified regarding preparation and academic achievement.  
The University also offers exceptionally well-prepared students the  flexibility  to  create  a  customised  major.  Such  a  student may opt to undertake an Interdisciplinary Major (instead of a pre-defined major) or a Self-designed Major.  
Students must initiate a request for an interdisciplinary major and  defend  a  proposal  before  a  Committee  on  Individual Choice. If approved, the programme of study will be designed by  the  Committee  on  Individual  Choice  in  conjunction  with the  student.  An  interested  student  should  apply  to  a  single major  at  the  time  of  application  and  may  indicate  their interest in an Interdisciplinary Major. The student will work with the Committee on Individual Choice during their first year at the  University,  designing  the  interdisciplinary  major  and  its accompanying programme of study by the end of the second semester. Alternatively, students may apply to transfer to an interdisciplinary major at any time; however, this may require additional  courses,  potentially  increasing  the  programme's duration. (Note that this option is in addition to the pre-designed interdisciplinary majors at the University, such as Philosophy, History and Languages, or Social and Political Sciences in the Bachelor of Arts Programme, or Finance and Economics in the Bachelor of Science in Management Programme.)
ground_truth: Yes, students are allowed to change their majors, including moving to a major hosted by a different School, subject to eligibility requirements and with the approval of the Dean of the School hosting the new major.

contexts:
## What does the Programme Office do?  
The Programme Office is the first stop for any query regarding your academic  programme.  The  Programme  Chair  and the  Programme  Manager  will  guide  you  throughout  your educational  journey  and  will  advise  you  on  several  issues, including:  
- Course registration and providing advice for the planning of course credits
- Class scheduling
- Issuing various testimonials like no-objection certificates, bonafide, migration/ transference certificates, transcripts, etc., and attestation of various documents
- Registration for various government schemes for students, like distribution of electronic tablets
- Processing applications for government scholarships
- Educational verification for recruitment and further education
- Maintaining student records  
In  addition,  each  major  has  a  major  advisor  (a  faculty member) who advises students about the academic requirements of each major and helps them find appropriate courses  and  equivalents  to  meet  the  requirements  of  the major successfully.  
First-year students are a part of the University's Circle of Care initiative.  Each  Circle  is  led  by  a  Faculty  Advisor,  who  helps address the queries of new students, assists students transit from a school environment to a University, and acts as a guide who  can  advise  in  general  or  create  linkages  for  the  new student to access other resources.  
These multilevel resources form the Student Advising System, which helps you navigate the educational experience as best as possible.  
- Miscellaneous receipts
- Processing applications for academic leaves
- Applying for grade drops and processing results
- Resolving examination-related queries
- Single point of contact for student-related queries
- Communicating various informative notices to students
- Processing student requests for programme change
- Processing admission cancellations  
And much more!

## Academic Advising and Programme Office  
The  Undergraduate  College  and  the  various  Schools  at  the University  administer  the  programmes.  The  Deans  of  the Undergraduate  College  (the  Dean  and  the  Assistant  Dean) oversee the undergraduate learning experience, notably the Foundation Programme. The Deans of the Schools (the Deans and the Associate Deans) are responsible for the programmes and  the  majors,  the  delivery  of  courses,  and  the  relevant academic and co-curricular experiences.  
A  Programme  Office  manages  each  programme,  led  by  a Programme  Chair  (a  faculty  member)  and  a  Programme Manager (an administrative staff member). The Programme Office addresses all academic issues associated with a course or  a  programme  (for  example,  examinations,  attendance, grade sheets, etc.).

## Office of International Affairs  
The Office enhances and expands the University's internationalisation agenda by cultivating a culture of global citizenship and leadership at the University. Integrating a global perspective is critical to a high-impact 21st-century education. We work on deepening collaborations with global universities to enable our students, scholars, and faculty to get exposure to international learning and opportunities. The graduating batch secured admissions to leading universities in the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, and Australia. The Office offers the following services for advising students on opportunities at international Universities:  
- Informational  Sessions  and  Fairs: The  Office  regularly  conducts  informational  sessions  on  various  international  education opportunities by inviting experts, foreign universities, and foreign missions to the campus. The Office hosts an annual fair where students interact directly with university representatives from the most prestigious institutions.
- Study Abroad Programmes: The Office forges partnerships with global universities for semester abroad programmes, enabling a  select  group  of  students  to  experience  an  international  academic  environment  outside  India.  The  Office  announces  these opportunities to students, ensures an unbiased and comprehensive selection process, and subsequently supports students until their departure.
- International Student Support: The Office is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment for international students to thrive within Ahmedabad University and add to the campus's intellectual vitality. The Office helps students complete their registration formalities with the Foreign Regional Registration Office (FRRO) of the Government of India.
- Scholarship Alerts: The Office also regularly announces different scholarship and fellowship opportunities for studying abroad. It also informs students about short-term opportunities, like summer schools, with our partner universities.
- Workshops: The  Office  organises  workshops  and  talks  for  students,  guiding  them  toward  interaction  with  experts  who  deeply understand international education. In addition, the Office organises workshops wherein faculties from international institutions provide a hands-on learning experience for students.

## Course Registration Procedure  
Students must follow the steps below for course registration: Step  1:  Students  must  log  in  to  www.auris.ahduni.edu.in  with the credentials provided.  
Step 2: Click on the course registration tab.  
- Course registration will not open unless you have paid the fees and other dues. If you have paid the fees and cannot register,  contact  your  Programme  Office.  The  office  will verify the payment status and facilitate the registration.
- You can see the available courses from all Schools.
- You  can  search  for  any  course  at  any  School.  Through the course search facility, you can search for courses by course  codes,  course  names,  professors'  names,  course  
description keywords, etc.  
- Once you find the course of your choice, you can see the course details along with the available time slots.
- You  can  add  a  course  in  your  preferred  time  slot.  The system  will  check  the  time  of  your  previously  added courses, and if no clash is found, the course will be added to your course selection panel. The system will also check the maximum and minimum credit range you are allowed.
- You  can  choose  three  courses  beyond  your  maximum credit load as a preference.
- You can Add Week and Drop Month any courses to and from  your  course  selection  panel  during  the  course registration.
- You can repeat this process any number of times as long as the course registration system remains open.
- After  the  course  registration  system  is  closed,  the  last course  choice  submission  will  be  considered  your  final selection,  and  courses  will  be  allotted  based  on  that selection.

question: How many days does the specially curated ISP courses last?
answer: The specially curated ISP courses last for two weeks.
contexts:
## Independent Study Period  
The  Independent  Study  Period,  or  ISP,  is  a  mini-term  in December wherein students engage in activities and courses that  are  not  a  part  of  the  regular  semester.  These  activities range from specially curated ISP courses, apprenticing with a faculty on the student's research project, or an independent research  project  developed  by  the  student  and  supervised by  a  faculty.  Students  earn  credits  for  this  work,  which  may be  counted  towards  the  GERS,  Free  Electives,  or  even  Major Electives. Some students take an ISP simply because they want to participate in this highly creativity-oriented and reflective activity.  The  specially  curated  ISP  courses  signify  a  unique experience  and  are  conducted  in  studio  mode.  The  student undergoes this  course  one  at  a  time  over  two  weeks.  These courses are designed around a passion, are interdisciplinary, are  experiential  and  project-based,  and  build  conceptual knowledge. ISP ends with an Expo where all course students display their work to the University community and the public.  
Students  often  have  diverse  passions  but  no  means  to explore  them,  especially  when  they  are  unrelated  to  their academic specialisations. The ISP thus offers an opportunity to  explore  these  interests  beyond  the  classroom,  making learning very hands-on, collaborative, and intimate. Classrooms  become  studios,  and  thinking  out-of-the-box is  replaced  with  re-imagining  the  box.  These  courses  are offered in an eight-hour day format on 15 consecutive days, enabling  concentrated  learning  for  students  through  block courses, studio-inspired experiential courses, perspective, skill-building,  field  courses,  and  innovative  experiments  in learning.  ISP  is  an  amazingly  creative  campus  period  and embodies the Ahmedabad learning spirit.

question: What is the storage quota limit for alumni email IDs on the University domain?
answer: The storage quota limit for alumni email IDs on the University domain is 1 GB.
contexts:
## Campus IT Network, Technology Support, and Instructional Technology  
Students  at  the  University  have  access  to  various  technology resources.  Wired  and  wireless  network  connections,  including high-speed  internet  connectivity  centrally  managed  through  a firewall,  are  provided  across  the  University  campus.  The  Wi-Fi network  is  an  advanced  controller-based  system  with  wireless access points distributed across the campus, covering classrooms, laboratories, meeting rooms, the library, and the canteen, among other locations. Classrooms and laboratories have LAN and Wi-Fi connectivity.  Each  library  is  equipped  with  computer  resources. Our campus has adequate CCTV coverage to ensure safety and security.  
Students at Ahmedabad University are allotted individual email IDs with a storage quota limit of 10 GB on the University domain (that is,  username@ahduni.edu.in).  Similarly,  alumni  have  access  to their emails with a storage limit of 1 GB. Various email groups have been created  to  facilitate  communication  and  share  necessary information across varsity Schools.  
Additionally, the University uses KOHA for Library Management and Moodle as the Learning Management System. The University provides plagiarism  check  services  to  all  its  users  through  the  renowned  Turnitin  software.  Enterprise-level  endpoint  security  from  eScan  is deployed for all computer systems across the University and is also made available to students for installation (one device per student).  
We encourage using open-source and licensed software at the University and discourage using unlicensed software on campus. Online subscriptions  are  available  for  various  software,  including  MATLAB,  AutoCAD,  Turnitin,  Cadence,  National  Instruments  LabVIEW,  Solid Works, ANSYS, and LaserVCAD CNC train, among many others.  
Students also use various open-source software and application platforms like Ubuntu, OpenOffice, Audacity, Wireshark, Hadoop, Python, MAESTRO element, Chem Doodle, PHP, and Active Perl and are encouraged to try other open-source software.

question: What type of formalities does the Office of International Affairs help international students complete?
answer: The Office of International Affairs helps international students complete their registration formalities with the Foreign Regional Registration Office (FRRO) of the Government of India.
contexts:
## Office of International Affairs  
The Office enhances and expands the University's internationalisation agenda by cultivating a culture of global citizenship and leadership at the University. Integrating a global perspective is critical to a high-impact 21st-century education. We work on deepening collaborations with global universities to enable our students, scholars, and faculty to get exposure to international learning and opportunities. The graduating batch secured admissions to leading universities in the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, and Australia. The Office offers the following services for advising students on opportunities at international Universities:  
- Informational  Sessions  and  Fairs: The  Office  regularly  conducts  informational  sessions  on  various  international  education opportunities by inviting experts, foreign universities, and foreign missions to the campus. The Office hosts an annual fair where students interact directly with university representatives from the most prestigious institutions.
- Study Abroad Programmes: The Office forges partnerships with global universities for semester abroad programmes, enabling a  select  group  of  students  to  experience  an  international  academic  environment  outside  India.  The  Office  announces  these opportunities to students, ensures an unbiased and comprehensive selection process, and subsequently supports students until their departure.
- International Student Support: The Office is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment for international students to thrive within Ahmedabad University and add to the campus's intellectual vitality. The Office helps students complete their registration formalities with the Foreign Regional Registration Office (FRRO) of the Government of India.
- Scholarship Alerts: The Office also regularly announces different scholarship and fellowship opportunities for studying abroad. It also informs students about short-term opportunities, like summer schools, with our partner universities.
- Workshops: The  Office  organises  workshops  and  talks  for  students,  guiding  them  toward  interaction  with  experts  who  deeply understand international education. In addition, the Office organises workshops wherein faculties from international institutions provide a hands-on learning experience for students.

The Family Business and Entrepreneurship programme provides a platform for students to gain global business acumen through short-term  courses  at  foreign  universities,  exploring  international  business  nuances. ## Registration  
Students  must  pay  the  fees  with  all  dues  before  the  final registration  starts  or  by  the  due  date  mentioned  by  the University. Courses chosen during the pre-registration phase will be reserved until the payment of the fee or the due date. If not paid by the due date, courses will be removed from the student's  selection,  and  they  will  need  to  re-register  for  the courses. Final registration for each academic year starts in  
July  for  the  Monsoon  Semester,  in  December  for  the  Winter Semester,  and  in  the  last  week  of  May  for  the  Summer Semester.  In  October,  students  register  for  the  Independent Study Programme.

## Summer Internship  
A summer internship offers invaluable professional experience and  the  chance  to  develop  specific,  employable  skills.  It provides an excellent opportunity to explore potential career paths post graduation or discover new graduate programme interests. This experience connects with industry, often serving as a direct stepping stone to a job offer. Most of our programmes include a mandatory internship requirement.

## For an Existing Student  
A  student  who  has  enrolled  for  courses  at  Ahmedabad University and wishes to withdraw their admission must fill out the Application for Withdrawal of Admission, available online in  the  Ahmedabad  University  Resource  Information  System (AURIS). The student must take a printout of the form and get it  signed  by  the  concerned  personnel  from  the  Programme Office  for  'No  Dues'  and  submit  it  to  the  Programme  Office between  9:00  am  and  6.00  pm  on  any  working  day.  The application will be processed within 15 working days from the date of its receipt.

## Process Flow for How to Apply for Authorised Absence  
- Students must apply in advance on AURIS under Quick Links Authorised  Absence  with  all  the  supporting  documents, such as an acceptance for a conference, sporting event, etc.,  along  with  a  supporting  letter  from  the  concerned University  authority  (professor,  Director  of  Sports)  if  the absence  is  related  to  a  University  activity  or  promoted by the University, as early as possible before the dates of absence.
- The  concerned  Programme  Office  receives  the  email intimation and application via AURIS and forwards it to the Programme Chair.
- The Chair will then relay the application with their recommendation  to  the  School  Dean.  She  may  consult others  at  the  University  to  ascertain  the  application's genuineness.
- Upon  verifying  the  application,  the  Programme  Chair will  inform the Programme Office and the student of the Dean's decision.
- If  approved,  an  email  notification  will  be  sent  to  the student,  Programme  Chair,  Programme  Manager,  and course instructors of the concerned student. The student should also intimate all the concerned instructors whose sessions they will miss.  
Note that if the Authorised Absence is approved, the student will  be  marked  as  AA  for  the  missed  session,  and  this  will be  treated  as  equivalent  to  being  present  to  calculate  the number of absences.

## Amit Mehta  
Associate Director, Management Programmes Office of Graduate Admissions and Outreach

## Grade Point Average  
A Grade Point Average, or a GPA, is a weighted average of the grades you obtained during a semester, while a Cumulative Grade Point Average, or a CGPA, indicates the weighted average of the grades you obtained in all the courses that you have taken to date.  
Each Major provides a sample study plan to help you design your own. You do not have to follow the sample plan but must build  one  for  yourself,  keeping  your  needs  and  interests  in mind.  
Details of each course are provided in a Course Outline, which includes  a  description  of  the  course,  learning  outcomes,  a session plan, assessment component and pattern of grading, reference material, etc. You should carefully understand each course outline and refer to it regularly as the course progresses, as it will carry many instructions for the programme.

## Transfer of Credits  
Coursework  undertaken  at  a  university/institute  other  than  Ahmedabad  University  may  be  considered  towards  the  degree requirement at Ahmedabad. The credits earned by a student outside Ahmedabad University will be transferred based on the academic rigour of  the  coursework  done  elsewhere.  Not  all  coursework  undertaken  outside  Ahmedabad  may  be  eligible  for consideration. Students should consult their Programme Chairs to understand whether their coursework outside Ahmedabad qualifies for credit transfer and the proportion of credits they will transfer.  
The University will not include the transferred credits and grades in the calculation of the CGPA. However, if earned successfully, such credits will be counted towards the total credit requirement for graduation.

## Grade drop structure with number of absences*

## Grade drop structure with number of absences*  
Important: If a student will get a penalty due to absences arising out of unavoidable reasons such as long medical illness, family tragedy, etc., they may approach their Programme Chair with the relevant information. Students are advised that the 25 per cent absence without any penalty as per the Attendance Policy is meant to be used for exigencies. Frivolous absences may disqualify them from any penalty relaxation if and when a genuine reason arises.  
For the Foundation Programme, students are expected to attend 100 per cent of the sessions in the studios and the first-year seminar courses. If a student's attendance in a course falls below 75 per cent of all sessions of a studio or a seminar course or falls below 60 per cent in any module (weekly unit), they will receive an 'NP' grade in the studio/seminar course.  
<!-- image -->

## Attendance Policy  
Our academic programme relies on rigorous classroom engagement.; consequently, missed classes compromise learning not fully captured by examinations. Therefore, significant absences will result in lower grades, reflecting compromised learning. We encourage students to attend 100 per cent of the sessions. However, in case of emergencies, no penalty is imposed as long as a student attends at least 75 per cent of all sessions in a course.  
However, if a student's attendance in a course falls below 75 per cent, one grade drop will be applied. Further, if the attendance falls below 60 per cent, the student will receive an 'NP' grade and have to repeat the course. This policy encourages students to maintain a high level of attendance and ensure that learning is not compromised.

## Add Week and Drop Month  
The University encourages you to attend a few course sessions during  the  first  month  of  classes  to  decide  if  the  course interests you. This month is known as the Add Week and Drop Month. Add Week and Drop Month give flexibility to students who have attended the initial sessions of a course and wish to drop a course they registered for earlier and instead register for another course of their choice, if available. You are advised to attend sessions in the courses you are interested in during the Add Week and Drop Month, even if you have not registered for  them.  This  will  help  prevent  loss  of  attendance  in  case the  said  course  is  allotted  to  you  subsequently  based  on availability. Once the Add Week and Drop Month is over, you cannot drop any course you have registered for. However, if a student wishes to do so due to exceptional circumstances, they may only do so with the approval of the Programme Chair, the Associate Dean, or the Dean of the School to which they belong. The request may be approved or rejected based on the circumstances and the explanation offered by the student.

For withdrawal of admission and seeking a refund of fees, the candidate  must  submit  the  original  fee  receipt,  admission offer  letter,  and  other  documents  received  at  the  time  of admission. Student/applicant must fill in the letter of request for withdrawal duly signed by them and the Parent/Guardian thereof. The letter is available at the Office of Admissions and Financial Aid and can be filled and submitted at the time of seeking withdrawal, along with the required documents.
ground_truth: Refund up to 30 days from commencement → 50% of tuition fees.

## What does the Programme Office do?  
The Programme Office is the first stop for any query regarding your academic  programme.  The  Programme  Chair  and the  Programme  Manager  will  guide  you  throughout  your educational  journey  and  will  advise  you  on  several  issues, including:  
- Course registration and providing advice for the planning of course credits
- Class scheduling
- Issuing various testimonials like no-objection certificates, bonafide, migration/ transference certificates, transcripts, etc., and attestation of various documents
- Registration for various government schemes for students, like distribution of electronic tablets
- Processing applications for government scholarships
- Educational verification for recruitment and further education
- Maintaining student records  
In  addition,  each  major  has  a  major  advisor  (a  faculty member) who advises students about the academic requirements of each major and helps them find appropriate courses  and  equivalents  to  meet  the  requirements  of  the major successfully.  
First-year students are a part of the University's Circle of Care initiative.  Each  Circle  is  led  by  a  Faculty  Advisor,  who  helps address the queries of new students, assists students transit from a school environment to a University, and acts as a guide who  can  advise  in  general  or  create  linkages  for  the  new student to access other resources.  
These multilevel resources form the Student Advising System, which helps you navigate the educational experience as best as possible.  
- Miscellaneous receipts
- Processing applications for academic leaves
- Applying for grade drops and processing results
- Resolving examination-related queries
- Single point of contact for student-related queries
- Communicating various informative notices to students
- Processing student requests for programme change
- Processing admission cancellations  
And much more!
## Course Registration

## Course Registration Procedure  
Students must follow the steps below for course registration: Step  1:  Students  must  log  in  to  www.auris.ahduni.edu.in  with the credentials provided.  
Step 2: Click on the course registration tab.  
- Course registration will not open unless you have paid the fees and other dues. If you have paid the fees and cannot register,  contact  your  Programme  Office.  The  office  will verify the payment status and facilitate the registration.
- You can see the available courses from all Schools.
- You  can  search  for  any  course  at  any  School.  Through the course search facility, you can search for courses by course  codes,  course  names,  professors'  names,  course  
description keywords, etc.  
- Once you find the course of your choice, you can see the course details along with the available time slots.
- You  can  add  a  course  in  your  preferred  time  slot.  The system  will  check  the  time  of  your  previously  added courses, and if no clash is found, the course will be added to your course selection panel. The system will also check the maximum and minimum credit range you are allowed.
- You  can  choose  three  courses  beyond  your  maximum credit load as a preference.
- You can Add Week and Drop Month any courses to and from  your  course  selection  panel  during  the  course registration.
- You can repeat this process any number of times as long as the course registration system remains open.
- After  the  course  registration  system  is  closed,  the  last course  choice  submission  will  be  considered  your  final selection,  and  courses  will  be  allotted  based  on  that selection.
